decision_summary: Constraints are satisfied with a significant margin (58.6°C vs 85.0°C limit). Following the strategy shift suggestion to prefer material tuning as geometry changes hit limits, I will slightly increase the heat spreader conductivity to further reduce the objective temperature while staying well within the allowed 2.0x step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the peak chip temperature. The change (1.2x) is within the allowed single-step ratio of 2.0x.

expected_effects: Improved thermal uniformity across the spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity increases
Material property may approach physical realism limits if increased too aggressively in future steps
